mnemonics: BFFs (Boolean, Fuzzy, Extended Boolean) are the alternative set-theoretic models for IR.
keyPoints: Standard Boolean Model: Uses exact matching with AND, OR, NOT operations.
Fuzzy Set Model: Documents belong to a set to a certain degree, allowing for partial matches and ranking.
Extended Boolean Model: Incorporates elements of the vector model (like term weights) into the Boolean framework to allow for ranking and partial matching.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Fuzzy Set Model with the Vector Space Model; Fuzzy is set-theoretic and deals with degrees of membership, while Vector is algebraic and uses proximity in space.
Assuming the Standard Boolean Model can rank results; it only produces a simple yes/no (crisp set) match and cannot order results by relevance.
